ops.title: iOS: hoist uncommitted block drafts into BoardStore (survive view-identity churn)
ops.description: The sticky-section-headers change (task dd4aa844) nests block identity beneath group identity in BoardScreen's board: `ForEach(sectionGroups(currentBlocks))` → `Section` → `ForEach(group.blocks)`. When a live doc change alters section structure (a `doc.replaced` that inserts/removes/moves a `.section`), following blocks migrate between groups, their SwiftUI identity path changes, and local `@State` resets — losing an input field's uncommitted `draft` (`InputBlockView.swift` `@State draft/committed`), an open feedback composer and its text (`ApprovalBlockView.swift` `@State composing/draft`), detail/markdown expansion (`DetailView`, `MarkdownText`), and a pack webview's measured height (back to the 140pt default). The pre-grouping flat `ForEach(currentBlocks, id: \.id)` preserved all of this across restructures, and the web SPA remains immune (its sticky sections are flat siblings, `BoardBlocks.tsx`). Accepted at ship time as the cost of SwiftUI's Section-based pinning; the durable fix is hoisting transient drafts into `BoardStore` keyed by block id (draft text + composer-open flags at minimum; expansion state judgment call) so identity churn can't drop user-typed content. Surfaced by the adversarial review of the sticky-headers diff.

ops.text: An uncommitted input draft and an open approval feedback composer (with text) survive a doc.replaced that inserts a new section above their blocks, proven by a test or scripted sim repro
Composer focus/dismiss behavior and input commit flow are unchanged for the no-restructure path (existing tests stay green)
